sections.paragraph:
φυλαϰή, ῆς, ἡ [φυλαϰός Ionic form of φύλαξ q.v.]

—1. ‘a place for detaining a law-breaker’, not a prison for carrying out a specified period of detention, but a place in which one is confined until disposition is made of the detainee’s case, holding cell, jail, prison Mt 5:25; 14:3, 10; 25:36; Lk 21:12; J 3:24; Ac 5:19; 16:24; 26:10 and oft. in Lk and Ac; 2 Cor 6:5; Hb 11:36; 1 Pt 3:19 (the ‘spirits’ are confined while awaiting their judgment); Rv 2:10; 18:2; 20:7.

—2. ‘a sentry station with a contingent of guards’, guard-post Ac 12:10.

—3. ‘period of time for mounting guard’, watch

—a. specifically of the Roman method Mt 14:25; Mk 6:48; Lk 12:38.

—b. non-specific Mt 24:43; Lk 2:8.
